tracks.title: Computer Science Foundations
tracks.tagline: A complete CS1 path for learning programming from first principles.
tracks.description: An original Python-first introductory computer science course built around skills, lessons, checks, and spaced review.

courses.title: CS1: Introduction to Programming with Python
courses.tagline: From program state to functions, collections, files, testing, algorithms, recursion, and objects.
courses.description: A standard first-semester CS1 course for beginners. The course emphasizes reading code, tracing state, decomposing problems, testing behavior, and building small Python programs with confidence.

modules.title: Program State and Execution
modules.summary: Learn how Python programs run, store values, evaluate expressions, and communicate through the console.

modules.title: Conditions and Choice
modules.summary: Use booleans, comparisons, and branches to make programs react to data.

modules.title: Iteration and Patterns
modules.summary: Master repeated execution with while loops, for loops, accumulators, sentinels, and nested loops.

modules.title: Functions and Decomposition
modules.summary: Break programs into named pieces with parameters, return values, scope, and small designs.

modules.title: Strings and Text Processing
modules.summary: Treat text as structured data with indexing, slicing, searching, and parsing patterns.

modules.title: Lists and Sequence Processing
modules.summary: Use lists and sequence traversal to store, search, transform, and summarize many values.

modules.title: Dictionaries and Structured Data
modules.summary: Represent lookup tables, counts, groups, and simple records with dictionaries and nested data.

modules.title: Files and Data Summaries
modules.summary: Move from small examples to real inputs by reading files, parsing rows, and reporting summaries.

modules.title: Testing and Debugging
modules.summary: Read errors, design useful tests, and isolate bugs with evidence instead of guesses.

modules.title: Robust Programs
modules.summary: Handle bad input, expected failures, and edge cases without hiding defects.

modules.title: Algorithms and Efficiency
modules.summary: Recognize simple algorithms and compare how much work different approaches do.

modules.title: Recursion
modules.summary: Understand recursive definitions through base cases, smaller inputs, and traceable returns.

modules.title: Objects and Integration
modules.summary: Use simple classes for state and plan a small multi-function program from requirements.

skills.title: Read sequential program flow
skills.description: Can trace a short Python program from top to bottom and predict the order of effects.

skills.title: Track variable state
skills.description: Can determine the value of variables after assignments and reassignments.

skills.title: Evaluate Python expressions
skills.description: Can predict the result of simple arithmetic and string expressions.

skills.title: Identify basic Python types
skills.description: Can distinguish common values such as integers, floats, strings, and booleans.

skills.title: Predict console input and output
skills.description: Can follow how input enters a program and how print statements produce output.

skills.title: Construct program state snapshots
skills.description: Can write a compact table of variable names and values at a marked point in execution.

skills.title: Evaluate boolean expressions
skills.description: Can evaluate comparisons and compound boolean expressions for concrete values.

skills.title: Evaluate boolean branches
skills.description: Can predict which branch of an if/else decision will execute.

skills.title: Trace multi-way selection
skills.description: Can identify the first true branch in an if/elif/else chain.

skills.title: Guard invalid input
skills.description: Can use conditions to separate valid input from impossible or unsupported cases.

skills.title: Trace simple loops
skills.description: Can determine loop iterations and final state in simple examples.

skills.title: Trace range-based loops
skills.description: Can predict the values produced by range and the number of loop body executions.

skills.title: Build accumulator and counter loops
skills.description: Can recognize and trace loops that compute totals, counts, and running values.

skills.title: Trace sentinel-controlled while loops
skills.description: Can trace loops that continue until a stopping value or condition appears.

skills.title: Trace nested loops
skills.description: Can separate outer-loop progress from inner-loop work in small examples.

skills.title: Trace built-in function calls
skills.description: Can distinguish function arguments, returned values, and printed output for common built-ins.

skills.title: Trace user-defined functions
skills.description: Can follow control into a function body and back to the caller.

skills.title: Use parameters and return values
skills.description: Can map arguments to parameters and predict returned values for small functions.

skills.title: Distinguish local scope
skills.description: Can tell which names exist inside a function and which names belong to the caller.

skills.title: Design small Python functions
skills.description: Can describe a function's purpose, inputs, output, and representative tests before implementation.

skills.title: Index and slice strings
skills.description: Can access characters and substrings by position.

skills.title: Parse text with string methods
skills.description: Can use simple string methods to search, split, and clean text data.

skills.title: Trace list operations
skills.description: Can follow indexing, append, replacement, and simple list mutation.

skills.title: Iterate over sequences
skills.description: Can visit each item in a string or list in a controlled order.

skills.title: Search and aggregate sequences
skills.description: Can combine traversal with counting, summing, membership checks, and simple searches.

skills.title: Update lists safely
skills.description: Can choose when to mutate a list in place and when to build a new list.

skills.title: Use dictionary lookup
skills.description: Can read and update key-value mappings for direct lookup tasks.

skills.title: Build frequency tables
skills.description: Can count occurrences by updating dictionary entries during traversal.

skills.title: Model records with nested data
skills.description: Can represent simple records with dictionaries and lists of dictionaries.

skills.title: Read text file lines
skills.description: Can reason about reading a file line by line and cleaning newline characters.

skills.title: Parse delimited records
skills.description: Can split simple rows into fields and summarize the resulting records.

skills.title: Classify Python errors
skills.description: Can identify common error categories from symptoms or short traceback excerpts.

skills.title: Design small test cases
skills.description: Can choose normal, boundary, and revealing examples for a small function.

skills.title: Isolate bugs with hypotheses
skills.description: Can approach a bug with reproduce-isolate-inspect instead of guessing.

skills.title: Handle simple exceptions
skills.description: Can identify when a small try/except block is appropriate and what it should protect.

skills.title: Reason about edge cases
skills.description: Can identify empty, minimal, maximum, and unusual inputs that may break a program.

skills.title: Reason about linear search
skills.description: Can trace a search that checks sequence items one at a time.

skills.title: Reason about sorting
skills.description: Can describe what sorted output requires and compare simple sorted results.

skills.title: Compare simple runtime growth
skills.description: Can compare how work changes when input size grows in common beginner patterns.

skills.title: Identify recursive base cases
skills.description: Can identify the condition that stops a recursive function from calling itself forever.

skills.title: Trace simple recursion
skills.description: Can follow a recursive function through smaller calls and returning values.

skills.title: Model state with simple classes
skills.description: Can explain how object attributes store related state and how methods update that state.

skills.title: Plan a multi-function program
skills.description: Can split a small project into data representation, helper functions, main flow, and tests.

lessons.title: Read sequential programs
lessons.summary: Program order matters. Python executes one statement, updates state or output, then moves to the next statement unless control flow says otherwise.
lessons.objective: Trace a short Python script from first line to last line and predict its visible output.

sections.title: Core idea
body: Program order matters. Python executes one statement, updates state or output, then moves to the next statement unless control flow says otherwise.
Program order matters. Python executes one statement, updates state or output, then moves to the next statement unless control flow says otherwise.
sections.checkpoint: Which value or state change determines the next step?

sections.title: Reasoning method
body: Start with one concrete input or state. Write down intermediate values before deciding the final result.
Compare what each statement reads, what it changes, and whether it returns or prints a value.
sections.checkpoint: What intermediate value would catch the most common mistake here?

workedExamples.prompt: print("ready"); print("go")
steps: The first print statement displays ready.
The second print statement displays go.
Nothing runs out of order unless the code explicitly changes control flow.

assessments.title: Entry diagnostic
assessments.description: Checks early execution, state, branch, loop, and debugging readiness.

items.prompt: Which statement best describes sequential program flow?
options: The computer randomly picks a suitable line
The computer executes instructions in a defined order
The computer only executes the last line
items.correctAnswer: The computer executes instructions in a defined order
items.explanation: A sequential program follows the written execution order unless control flow changes it.

items.prompt: `x = 2`; then `x = x + 5`. What is the final value of `x`?
items.explanation: The old value 2 is read, 5 is added, and 7 is stored back into x.

items.prompt: If `score = 12` and the condition is `score > 10`, which branch runs?
options: The true branch
The false branch
Neither branch
items.correctAnswer: The true branch
items.explanation: 12 is greater than 10, so the condition is true.

items.prompt: `total = 0`; then a loop adds `1`, `2`, and `3`. What is the final value of `total`?
What is the best first move when a program gives the wrong answer?
options: Change many lines at once
Reproduce the bug and inspect the relevant state
Delete the whole module
items.correctAnswer: Reproduce the bug and inspect the relevant state
items.explanation: A reproducible case and visible state make debugging evidence-based.

assessments.title: Lesson check: Read sequential programs
assessments.description: Checks whether the learner can apply read sequential program flow.

items.prompt: Which habit best supports read sequential program flow?
options: Trace concrete values step by step
Guess from variable names only
Change unrelated code until output changes
items.correctAnswer: Trace concrete values step by step
items.explanation: Concrete tracing keeps reasoning tied to actual execution.

items.prompt: What should you record when predicting the next statement?
items.explanation: Recording state or values makes each step checkable.

assessments.title: Lesson check: Track variable state

The variable is deleted

items.explanation: The old value is read, one is added, and the result is stored again.
